title: The Art of Gathering
author: Priya Parker
subline: How we meet is why we meet.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A long table seen from above. 12 place settings: 11 graphite dots (attendees) and 1 red dot (the host / the purpose — the one who gives the gathering meaning). -->
  <g transform="translate(800 900)">
    <!-- The table: a long rounded rectangle outline -->
    <rect x="-700" y="-180" width="1400" height="360" rx="20" ry="20" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2"/>
    <!-- A second inner hairline for table depth -->
    <rect x="-680" y="-160" width="1360" height="320" rx="14" ry="14" fill="none" stroke="#1a1a1a" stroke-width="1"/>
    <!-- 12 place settings: 6 on top, 6 on bottom of the table, equally spaced -->
    <g>
      <!-- Top side -->
      <circle cx="-580" cy="-100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
      <circle cx="-380" cy="-100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
      <circle cx="-180" cy="-100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
      <circle cx="20" cy="-100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
      <circle cx="220" cy="-100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
      <circle cx="420" cy="-100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
      <!-- Bottom side -->
      <circle cx="-580" cy="100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
      <circle cx="-380" cy="100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
      <circle cx="-180" cy="100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
      <circle cx="20" cy="100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
      <circle cx="220" cy="100" r="32" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
      <!-- The host: red place setting, bottom-right -->
      <circle cx="420" cy="100" r="36" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Tiny name labels under each plate (initials) -->
    <g font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.04em" text-anchor="middle">
      <text x="-580" y="-50">A</text>
      <text x="-380" y="-50">B</text>
      <text x="-180" y="-50">C</text>
      <text x="20" y="-50">D</text>
      <text x="220" y="-50">E</text>
      <text x="420" y="-50">F</text>
      <text x="-580" y="170">G</text>
      <text x="-380" y="170">H</text>
      <text x="-180" y="170">I</text>
      <text x="20" y="170">J</text>
      <text x="220" y="170">K</text>
      <text x="420" y="170" fill="#fc1c46">HOST</text>
    </g>
    <!-- Top label -->
    <text x="0" y="-280" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">SEATING PLAN · 12 SEATS</text>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: A long table viewed from above with 12 place settings in graphite hairline circles, lettered A through K, with one red filled circle — the host, the person who gives the gathering purpose. The single red dot is the book's thesis made literal: without a host with a clear purpose, a gathering is just chairs. Below: 300px mixed-case 'The Art / of Gathering' title, weight 300, literary and human.
